perturbation.original_text: 4.1.1.5 Environmental Laws. Operator shall comply in all respects with all Environmental Laws and all Environmental Permits.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1.1.5 Environmental Laws. Operator shall endeavor to comply with Environmental Laws and Environmental Permits to the best of its ability.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall comply in all respects' to 'shall endeavor to comply...to the best of its ability,' the contract introduces ambiguity regarding the level of compliance required. This creates an in-text contradiction with Section 4.2 (Standard of Care), which states Operator shall perform its duties (iii) in accordance with all valid and applicable Laws, including, without limitation, all Environmental Laws and Environmental Permits.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.2 Standard of Care. Operator shall perform its duties and obligations hereunder and its responsibilities as Operator of the Gathering System, (i) in a good and workmanlike manner, (ii) in conformity with the good practices in the natural gas gathering industry, (iii) in accordance with all valid and applicable Laws, including, without limitation, all Environmental Laws and Environmental Permits, (iv) in accordance with the Subject Gathering Agreements, and (v) in accordance with the Proposed Operating Expense Budget.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the obligation from 'shall comply in all respects' to 'shall endeavor to comply...to the best of its ability'. This introduces a level of discretion in compliance that directly contradicts Section 4.2's requirement that the Operator 'shall perform its duties...in accordance with all valid and applicable Laws, including, without limitation, all Environmental Laws and Environmental Permits'. The original text mandated strict compliance, whereas the changed text allows for a weaker standard, creating a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5.2.1 Invoicing and Payment. Operator shall invoice Owner, monthly, for all costs and expenses incurred hereunder during the immediately preceding month and, without duplication, in any prior month. Owner shall pay all such amounts prior to the later of (a) 50 days after the end of the month in which the expenses were incurred or (b) 10 days after the date of receipt of each invoice. Invoices received after 1:00 pm local time will be deemed received on the next Business Day. If the day on which any payment is due is not a Business Day, then the relevant payment shall be due upon the immediately succeeding Business Day. Any amounts which remain due and owing after the due date shall bear interest at the lower of the prime rate as published in the "Money Rates" section of The Wall Street Journal plus two percent (2%) per annum and the maximum lawful rate of interest, compounded daily from the date such payment is due until such payment is made. Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, any indemnification payments required to be made by a Party hereunder shall be made to the indemnified Party upon receipt by the indemnifying Party of written demand therefor from the indemnified Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.2.1 Invoicing and Payment. Operator shall invoice Owner, monthly, for costs and expenses. Owner shall pay such amounts. Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, any indemnification payments required to be made by a Party hereunder shall be made to the indemnified Party upon receipt by the indemnifying Party of written demand therefor from the indemnified Party.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the detailed payment terms (50 days after month end or 10 days after invoice receipt) creates an in-text contradiction with Section 5.3.1, which assumes a defined payment schedule for calculating set-offs. The contradiction arises because Section 5.3.1 discusses set-off rights against 'payments required to be made hereunder by Operator,' implying a clear payment obligation which is now undefined due to the removed terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.3 Setoff. 5.3.1 All payments required to be made hereunder by Operator shall be (i) absolute and unconditional, (ii) calculated without reference to any netting, set-off, counterclaim or other right which Operator may have against Owner, including, without limitation, any right to set-off against any amounts owed by Owner under this Agreement or any other Transaction Document, and (iii) made free and clear of and without any deduction for or on account of any such netting, set-off or counterclaim.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined specific payment terms (50 days after the month end or 10 days after invoice), which provided a clear obligation for payment. The changed text removes these specific terms, leaving the payment schedule undefined. Section 5.3.1 refers to 'payments required to be made hereunder,' implying a definite payment obligation and timing, which is now absent in the modified Section 5.2.1. This creates a contradiction as the set-off clause relies on a defined payment schedule, which no longer exists, thereby negatively impacting enforcement and creating uncertainty.

perturbation.original_text: 9.13 Assignment. Operator shall not make any assignment of all or any part of this Agreement or any of the rights or obligations hereunder unless there first shall have been obtained the written consent thereto of Owner, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld, conditioned, or delayed. Owner shall not make any assignment of all or any part of this Agreement or any of the rights or obligations hereunder except in connection with the sale or conveyance of all or any part of the Gathering System. This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the successors and assigns of the Parties. Any attempted assignment of this Agreement in violation of this Section 9.13 shall be null and void.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.13 Assignment. This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the successors and assigns of the Parties.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clauses that restrict both Owner and Operator from assigning the contract without consent, the modified text creates an in-text contradiction with Section 9.15 (No Third Party Beneficiary). The original text restricts assignments, the modified version deletes this restriction. The 'No Third Party Beneficiary' clause aims to limit the benefits to only the parties involved (Owner and Operator). However, without the restrictions on assignment, the contract could be freely assigned to third parties, indirectly making them beneficiaries, which contradicts the intent of Section 9.15.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.15 No Third Party Beneficiary. Except for parties indemnified hereunder, this Agreement is for the sole benefit of the Parties and their respective successors and permitted assigns, and shall not inure to the benefit of any other Person whomsoever or whatsoever, it being the intention of the Parties that no third Person shall be deemed a third-party beneficiary of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly restricts assignment without consent from both parties. The changed text removes these restrictions, allowing for free assignment. This directly contradicts Section 9.15, which limits benefits to the original parties and their *permitted* assigns. 'Permitted' implies restrictions on assignment, which are now absent in the modified Section 9.13, thereby creating a contradiction because third parties could become beneficiaries, violating the intent of Section 9.15.
